mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, which directly compresses valuation multiples and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate reading above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, which compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal consumption expenditures erodes corporate profit margins, which directly drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where the CPI index exceeds 300, further increases in the federal funds rate trigger liquidity crunches that override rate-cut expectations, which directly produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly negative when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS rises, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for credit spread widening.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, which directly drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months consistently coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts reading below 1200 thousand units signals a sharp slowdown in construction activity and consumer confidence, which directly produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where housing starts breach the 1200 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while real GDP growth decelerates indicates supply chain bottlenecks and weak aggregate demand, which directly precedes earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate remains below 4.0% while the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the labor market resilience to tight monetary policy supports wage growth and consumer spending, which directly drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive when both conditions hold, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.
